questions.explanation: 2⁵ means 2 × 2 × 2 × 2 × 2, which equals 32. A common mistake is to calculate 2 × 5.
The square root of 81, written as √81, is the number that multiplies by itself to make 81. Since 9 × 9 = 81, the answer is 9.
When multiplying powers with the same base, you add the indices (exponents). So, 3² × 3⁴ = 3⁽²⁺⁴⁾ = 3⁶.
When dividing powers with the same base, you subtract the indices (exponents). So, 10⁶ ÷ 10² = 10⁽⁶⁻²⁾ = 10⁴.
Any non-zero number raised to the power of 0 is equal to 1.
When raising a power to another power, you multiply the indices (exponents). So, (2³)² = 2⁽³ˣ²⁾ = 2⁶.
A power of 1/2 is the same as the square root. Therefore, 9¹ᐟ² = √9 = 3.
A power of 1/3 is the same as the cube root. We are looking for a number that, when multiplied by itself three times, equals 27. 3 × 3 × 3 = 27.

questions.questionText: What is 2⁻³ written as a fraction?
questions.explanation: A negative power indicates the reciprocal. So, 2⁻³ = 1/2³ = 1/8. The negative sign in the power does not make the result negative.

questions.questionText: The area of a square is 64 cm². What is the length of one of its sides?
questions.explanation: The area of a square is side × side (side²). To find the side length from the area, you calculate the square root of the area. √64 = 8 cm.
10⁻³ is equal to 1/10³ or 1/1000. As a decimal, this is 0.001.
When multiplying powers with the same base, add the indices. 5 + (-2) = 3. So, the result is a³.
